claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 9 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and any children you're responsible for.

First, let me ask: **Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you please tell me:
- How many children?
- Their names and ages?

This will help me understand your situation and guide you through the eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. 

I have one child - my daughter Alex. She's 9 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know about Alex.

Now I need to ask you a few questions to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for her.

**First question: Are you responsible for Alex?** By this I mean, do you look after her and pay towards the costs of raising her (such as food, clothing, housing, etc.)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for Alex - I look after her every day and pay for everything she needs like food, clothes, school things, and our home. She lives with me full-time.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you for confirming that.

**Next question: Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training?**

I ask because at 9 years old, she's likely in primary school, which would count as full-time education. Could you confirm if she's attending school?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - she's in primary school. She goes to school full-time during the week.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

**Next question: Do you (or your partner if you have one) usually live in the UK?** 

This is important for determining Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you.

**Next question: Is there anyone else who might also be claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** 

For example, is there another parent or person who Alex lives with, or who might also consider themselves responsible for her?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there's no one else claiming Child Benefit for Alex. She lives with me and I'm the one looking after her.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

**One more important question: Is Alex currently in hospital or living in local authority care, a care home, or other residential care for more than 12 weeks (or expected to be)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. Alex lives at home with me and she's not in hospital or care or anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! Based on the information you've provided, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is under 16 years old (she's 9)
- You are responsible for her - you look after her and pay for her needs
- She lives with you in the UK (Manchester)
- She's in full-time education (primary school)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for her
- She's not in hospital or care

You meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. If you haven't already claimed, you can apply through the government website or by post. Child Benefit is currently paid at a rate that depends on whether the child is your first or subsequent child.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: 1) She is under 16 years old (she is 9 years old). 2) The claimant is responsible for Alex, looking after her and paying for her needs including food, clothing, and housing. 3) Alex lives with the claimant full-time in the UK (Manchester). 4) Alex is in full-time education (primary school). 5) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. 6) Alex is not in hospital or local authority care for more than 12 weeks. Therefore, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for one child, Alex (age 9), who lives with them in Manchester. Alex attends primary school full-time, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for her, and she is not in hospital or care. All eligibility criteria are met.

Therefore, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for one child, Alex (age 9), who lives with them in Manchester. Alex attends primary school full-time, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for her, and she is not in hospital or care. All eligibility criteria are met.
